evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis layout clearly shows loss (log scale, left) and cosine LR schedule (right) over 200 steps; color-coded axis labels and spine colors reinforce which line belongs to which axis.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype=42; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans — not selectable/editable vector text. Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1f77b4) for loss, red (#d62728) for LR — both colorblind-distinguishable; red is directional (decay) not categorical, consistent with brief hints.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6.5, 3.8) with tight_layout; no gaping whitespace, legend placed upper-right without overlap, y-range uses semilogy with auto-range appropriate to data.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; color coupling of ticks/labels is implemented as hinted; legend present. Minor issue: no explicit note on directional asymmetry of axes (brief hint about 'more is better' warning not addressed in caption).
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF exists but pdffonts shows Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans — text is not selectable/editable; pdf.fonttype=42 was not set in the script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial (PDF 17 KB, PNG 70 KB, SVG 44 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names both series, specifies axes and scale, gives specific numerical values (loss 2.19→0.21, LR 1e-3→0), and describes the cosine annealing schedule.

overall_strengths: Strong scientific communication: dual-axis design, color-coupled axis labels/spines, and a clear log-scale loss curve make the training dynamics immediately legible.
Excellent caption with specific numerical claims (loss 2.19→0.21, LR 1e-3→0) and correct description of both series.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 not set — the PDF contains Type 3 (bitmap) fonts (DejaVu Sans), so text is not selectable or editable in Illustrator/Inkscape, failing vector-fidelity requirements.
Default DejaVu Sans font used; no switch to a publication-quality font (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a serif), and the brief's typography hint is unaddressed.
